eng: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for English.
erg: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Sie.
erne: noun. A sea eagle (Haliaeetus), especially the white-tailed eagle (Haliaeetus albicilla)
fee: noun. An amount charged for a privilege.
feeing: verb. present participle and gerund of fee
feezing: verb. present participle and gerund of feeze
feign: verb. To make a false show or pretence of; to counterfeit or simulate.
fez: noun. A felt hat in the shape of a truncated cone, having a flat top with a tassel attached.
fie: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Fyer.
fig: noun. The fruit of the fig tree, pear -shaped and containing many small seeds.
fin: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Finnish.
fine: adjective. Senses referring to subjective quality.
finer: adjective. comparative form of fine: more fine
finger: noun. A slender jointed extremity of the human hand, (often) exclusive of the thumb, and the corresponding part, irrespective of including
fir: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Firan.
fire: noun. A (usually self-sustaining) chemical reaction involving the bonding of oxygen with carbon or other fuel, with the production of heat
firn: noun. A type of old snow which has gone through multiple thaw and refreeze cycles and thus is made of numerous small icy grains, though it
fiz: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Izere.
freeing: verb. present participle and gerund of free
frieze: noun. A kind of coarse woollen cloth or stuff with a shaggy or tufted (friezed) nap on one side.
gee: interjection. A general exclamation of surprise or frustration.
geez: interjection. An exclamation denoting surprise or frustration.
gen: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Geman Deng.
gene: noun. A theoretical unit of heredity of living organisms which may take several values and (in principle) predetermines a precise trait of
genie: noun. A jinn, a being descended from the jann, normally invisible to the human eye, but who may also appear in animal or human form.
genre: noun. A kind; a stylistic category or sort, especially of literature or other artworks.
gie: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Guébie.
gree: noun. One of a flight of steps.
green: noun. The color of grass and leaves; a primary additive color midway between yellow and blue which is evoked by light between roughly
grin: noun. A smile in which the lips are parted to reveal the teeth.
ire: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Iresim.
nee: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kumak.
neif: noun. A woman born in the state of serfdom; a female serf.
ree: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Rejang Kayan.
reef: noun. A chain or range of rocks, sand, or coral lying at or near the surface of the water.
reefing: noun. The process of reefing (taking in a sail); an act of reefing; also used of clothing.
refine: verb. To purify; reduce to a fine, unmixed, or pure state; to free from impurities.
reg: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kara (Tanzania).
rei: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Reli.
reign: noun. The exercise of sovereign power.
rein: noun. A strap or rope attached to a bridle or bit, used to control a horse, other animal or young child.

rig: noun. The rigging of a sailing ship or other such craft.
rin: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nungu.
ring: noun. A solid object in the shape of a circle.
zee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter Z / z.
zein: noun. A protein derived from corn / maize, having many industrial applications.
zig: noun. A sudden or sharp turn or change of direction.
zin: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Zinza.
zing: noun. A short high-pitched humming sound, such as that made by a bullet or vibrating string.
zinger: noun. A very rapidly moving object, especially one that is thrown.
